assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response clearly differentiates between dev clusters on Rapid channel and prod clusters on Stable channel throughout, with specific version verification commands for each environment type

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions mandatory resource requests, automatic node management by Autopilot, and includes commands to check for missing resource requests as Autopilot-specific concerns

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl commands for checking deprecated API usage and mentions GKE deprecation insights in the console

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All containers have resource requests (mandatory in Autopilot)' and provides a command to check for missing requests

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Response includes checks for PDBs, bare pods (stating 'No bare pods — all workloads managed by controllers'), and terminationGracePeriodSeconds for graceful shutdown

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Response includes specific gcloud commands to verify control plane version on both prod clusters after upgrade

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Response includes comprehensive workload health checks including stuck pods, deployment replica counts, StatefulSets status, and ingress/load balancer validation

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated 'Lessons from Dev Environment' section and specifically mentions using dev clusters on Rapid channel as a natural testing window before prod upgrades

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Response mentions in the final note that 1.33 will arrive in Stable ~1 month after Regular, indicating understanding of the release schedule timing beyond immediate notifications
Response includes comprehensive monitoring validation including baseline metrics, error rates, latency, resource utilization, and log flow verification

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Response only mentions maintenance exclusions for emergency deferral scenarios and focuses on cluster-level upgrade controls, not node-level maintenance, which is appropriate for Autopilot's managed node approach
